title: Debunking the Myth: Meat and Climate Change
subtitle: 肉类与气候变化的真相

paragraphs.en: As the scale and impacts of climate change become increasingly alarming, meat is a popular target for action. Advocates for the protection of the natural environment urge the public to eat less meat. Some activists have even called for taxing meat to reduce consumption of it. A key claim underlying these arguments holds that globally, meat production generates more greenhouse gases than the entire transportation sector.
paragraphs.zh: 随着气候变化的规模与影响日益令人警觉，肉类成为众矢之的。环保倡导者敦促公众少吃肉。一些活动人士甚至呼吁对肉类征税以减少消费。这些论点背后的一个关键主张是，全球肉类生产产生的温室气体比整个交通运输行业还多。

words.meaning: adj. 令人担忧的，惊人的

paragraphs.en: However, this claim is demonstrably wrong, as I will show. And its persistence has led to false assumptions about the linkage between meat and climate change. My recent research focuses on ways in which animal agriculture affects air quality and climate change. In my view, there are many reasons for either choosing animal protein or opting for a vegetarian selection.
paragraphs.zh: 然而我将证明，这一说法显然错误。其持续流传导致了对肉类与气候变化之间关系的错误假设。我近期的研究聚焦于畜牧业影响空气质量和气候变化的方式。在我看来，选择动物蛋白还是素食有许多理由。

paragraphs.en: However, abandoning meat and meat products is not the environmental panacea many would have us believe. And if taken to an extreme, it also could have harmful nutritional consequences. A healthy portion of meat's negative reputation centers on the assertion that livestock is the largest source of greenhouse gases worldwide.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，放弃肉类及肉制品并非许多人想让我们相信的环保万灵药。若走向极端，还可能带来有害的营养后果。肉类负面声誉的相当一部分源于牲畜是全球最大温室气体来源这一论断。

paragraphs.en: For example, an analysis published in 2009 by the World Watch Institute asserted that 51 percent of global greenhouse gas emissions come from rearing and processing livestock. According to the US Environmental Protection Agency, the largest sources of US greenhouse gas emissions in 2016 were electricity production (28 percent), transportation (28 percent) and industry (22 percent).
paragraphs.zh: 例如，世界观察研究所2009年发表的一项分析称，全球温室气体排放的51%来自牲畜饲养和加工。根据美国环保署的数据，2016年美国温室气体排放最大来源是电力生产（28%）、交通运输（28%）和工业（22%）。

paragraphs.en: All of agriculture accounted for a total of 9 percent, but all of animal agriculture contributes less than half of this amount, representing 3.9 percent of the total greenhouse emission in the US. That is very different from claiming that livestock represents as much as or more than transportation. Why is there such a misconception?
paragraphs.zh: 所有农业合计占9%，而畜牧业整体贡献不到其中一半，仅占美国温室气体排放总量的3.9%。这与声称牲畜排放相当于或超过交通运输的说法大相径庭。为何会有如此误解？

paragraphs.en: In 2006 the United Nations Food and Agriculture Organization published a study titled 'Livestock's Long Shadow,' which received widespread international attention. It stated that livestock produced a staggering 18 percent of the world's greenhouse gas emissions. The agency drew a startling conclusion that livestock was doing more to harm the climate than all modes of transportation combined.
paragraphs.zh: 2006年，联合国粮农组织发表了题为《牲畜的长影》的研究报告，引起国际广泛关注。报告称牲畜产生了惊人的全球温室气体排放量的18%。该机构得出了一个令人震惊的结论：牲畜对气候的危害超过所有运输方式的总和。

words.meaning: adj. 惊人的，令人震惊的

paragraphs.en: This latter claim was wrong, and has since been corrected by the report's senior author. The problem was that FAO analysts used a comprehensive life-cycle assessment for livestock, but a different method for transportation. For livestock, they considered every factor including fertilizer production, converting forests to pastures, growing feed, and direct animal emissions.
paragraphs.zh: 后一说法是错误的，此后已被报告主要作者纠正。问题在于粮农组织分析人员对牲畜采用了全面的生命周期评估，对交通运输却用了不同方法。对于牲畜，他们考虑了化肥生产、林地转牧场、饲料种植及动物直接排放等所有因素。

paragraphs.en: However, when they looked at transportation's carbon footprint, they ignored impacts from manufacturing vehicle materials, assembling vehicles and maintaining roads. Instead, they only considered exhaust from finished cars, trucks, trains and planes. As a result, the FAO's comparison was greatly distorted. I pointed out this flaw during a speech in 2010, which led to a flood of media coverage.
paragraphs.zh: 然而在考察交通运输碳足迹时，他们忽略了制造汽车材料、组装车辆及维护道路的影响，仅考虑了成品车、卡车、火车和飞机的尾气排放。结果粮农组织的比较严重失真。我在2010年一次演讲中指出这一缺陷，引发媒体大量报道。

paragraphs.en: To its credit, the FAO immediately owned up to its error. Unfortunately, the agency's initial claim had already received wide coverage. To this day, we struggle to unring the bell. In its most recent assessment, the FAO estimated that livestock produces 14.5 percent of global greenhouse gas emissions from human activities.
paragraphs.zh: 值得称赞的是，粮农组织立即承认了错误。不幸的是，该机构最初的说法已广为传播。时至今日，我们仍在努力消除影响。粮农组织在最新评估中估计，牲畜产生人类活动全球温室气体排放的14.5%。

paragraphs.en: There is no comparable full life-cycle assessment for transportation. However, as the report's senior author has pointed out, direct emissions from transportation versus livestock can be compared and amount to 14 versus 5 percent, respectively. Many people continue to think that avoiding meat even once a week will make a significant difference to the climate.
paragraphs.zh: 目前尚无针对交通运输的可比全生命周期评估。但如报告主要作者所指，交通运输与牲畜的直接排放可以比较，分别占14%和5%。许多人仍认为每周哪怕少吃一次肉也会对气候产生重大影响。

paragraphs.en: But according to one recent study, even if Americans eliminated all animal protein from their diets, they would reduce US greenhouse gas emissions by only 2.6 percent. According to our research at the University of California, Davis, if Meatless Monday were adopted by all Americans, we'd see a reduction of only 0.5 percent.
paragraphs.zh: 但根据最近一项研究，即便美国人从饮食中剔除所有动物蛋白，也仅能减少美国温室气体排放的2.6%。根据我们在加州大学戴维斯分校的研究，若所有美国人都践行“周一无肉”，减排幅度也仅0.5%。

paragraphs.en: Moreover, technological, genetic and management changes in US agriculture over the past 70 years have made livestock production more efficient and less greenhouse gas-intensive. According to FAO data, total direct greenhouse gas emissions from US livestock have declined by 11.3 percent since 1961, while livestock meat production has more than doubled.
paragraphs.zh: 此外，过去70年美国农业在技术、遗传和管理方面的变革使畜牧业生产更高效、温室气体强度更低。据粮农组织数据，自1961年以来美国牲畜直接温室气体排放总量下降了11.3%，而肉类产量却翻了一倍多。

paragraphs.en: Demand for meat is rising in developing and emerging economies, especially in the Middle East, North Africa and Southeast Asia. For example, raising livestock such as goats in Kenya is an important source of food and income for many small-scale farmers. But meat consumption per person in these regions still lags that of developed countries.
paragraphs.zh: 发展中经济体和新兴经济体对肉类的需求正在上升，尤其在中东、北非和东南亚。例如在肯尼亚，饲养山羊等牲畜是许多小农的重要食物和收入来源。但这些地区的人均肉类消费仍落后于发达国家。

paragraphs.en: In 2015, average annual meat consumption per person in developed countries was 92 kilograms, compared to 24 kilograms in the Middle East and North Africa. Given projected population growth, there will be opportunities for countries like the United States to bring sustainable livestock practices to the table. Removing animals from US agriculture would lower emissions only slightly and make it harder to meet nutritional requirements.
paragraphs.zh: 2015年，发达国家人均年肉类消费量为92公斤，而中东和北非为24公斤。考虑到预期人口增长，美国等国有机会推广可持续畜牧实践。将动物从美国农业中剔除仅能略微降低排放，却会使满足营养需求更加困难。

paragraphs.en: Many critics point out that growing only plants could produce more calories per person. But humans also need essential micro- and macro-nutrients. Moreover, not all plant parts are edible. Raising livestock adds nutritional and economic value to plant agriculture. The energy in plants that livestock consume is often in cellulose, indigestible for humans but breakable by ruminants like cows.
paragraphs.zh: 许多批评者指出，只种植作物能人均产出更多热量。但人类还需要必需的微量与宏量营养素。此外，并非所有植物部位都可食用。饲养牲畜为种植业增加了营养和经济价值。牲畜消耗的植物能量常以纤维素形式存在，人类无法消化，但牛等反刍动物可以分解。

paragraphs.en: According to the FAO, as much as 70 percent of global agricultural land is range land that can only be utilized as grazing land for ruminant livestock. The world population is projected to reach 9.8 billion by 2050. Feeding this many people will raise immense challenges. Meat is more calorie-dense per serving than vegetarian options, and ruminants thrive on feed unsuitable for humans.
paragraphs.zh: 据粮农组织称，全球多达70%的农业用地是只能用作反刍牲畜牧场的草地。预计到2050年世界人口将达98亿。养活这么多人将带来巨大挑战。每份肉类的热量密度高于素食，且反刍动物依靠不适合人类的饲料茁壮成长。

paragraphs.en: Raising livestock also provides much-needed income for small-scale farmers in developing nations. Worldwide, livestock provides a livelihood for 1 billion people. Climate change demands urgent attention, and the livestock industry has a large environmental footprint. These factors give us compelling reasons to continue working for greater efficiencies in animal agriculture. I believe the place to start is with science-based facts.
paragraphs.zh: 饲养牲畜还为发展中国家的小农提供了急需的收入。全球范围内，畜牧业为10亿人提供生计。气候变化亟待关注，畜牧业的环境足迹也很大。这些因素给了我们充分理由继续努力提高畜牧业效率。我相信应从基于科学的事实出发。
